macro_segments.title: Completion of Tzadi Alef: Machlon and Chilyon narrative and marketplace ethics

micro_segments.title: Review of Tzadi Alef discussion: market manipulation and abandonment of Israel
Debate of Rav and Shmuel on the names Machlon/Chilyon versus Yoash/Saraf
Interpretation of Machlon and Chilyon through etymology and sin
Baraita connecting Machlon/Chilyon to biblical narrative in Divrei Hayamim
Parsing of cryptic verse about Yokim, people of Chozeva, Yoash, Saraf, Moab and Lechem
Contrast between oath-keepers (Joshua, Rut, Rechabites) and oath-breakers (Machlon/Chilyon)
Continuation of verse parsing: Fashioners, plantings, Shlomo, Sanhedrin, Rut as mother of kingship
Story of Rechabites maintaining their father's oath and God's use as contrast to Israel
Baraita on produce of shemitah year and divine promise of sustained provision
Debates of Rav Nachman and Rav Sheshet on meanings of salmotone and rtzinta in context of grain preservation
Proof that old grain will sustain until new harvest reaches maturity
Teaching that aged grain improves with age and extends to all produce of shemitah
Final discussion on storage capacity and Rav Papa's caveat on which items improve with age

macro_segments.title: Introduction to Perek Hamocher Peirot and foundational case of selling seeds

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the sixth perek of Bava Batra on selling produce and conceptual framework
Mishnah case: seller of grain for planting denies responsibility for non-sprouting seeds
Clarification on flaxseed and caveat that Tosafot raises about seller's knowledge
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's position on non-edible vegetable seeds as seller responsibility

macro_segments.title: Debate of Rav and Shmuel on mekach taut and buyer's assumption in sale of goring ox

micro_segments.title: Case of selling a goring ox: Rav holds it is mekach taut, Shmuel holds seller can claim sale for meat
Gemara's examination of buyer's identity and profession to determine likely intention
Discussion of damim modiyim principle and whether price paid determines intended use
Case clarification when meat prices fluctuate and price cannot determine intended use
Gemara's question on practical difference between mekach taut and non-mekach taut when values equal
Answer that buyer seeks money back rather than reselling to butcher
Clarification that case requires seller to have cash available for reimbursement
Discussion of payment in kind versus cash and burden of locating alternative buyer

macro_segments.title: Framework of holchin bemamon achar harov and debate on burden of proof in monetary disputes

micro_segments.title: Rav's position: holchin bemamon achar harov when majority of buyers purchase for plowing
Shmuel's position: ayin holchin bemamon achar harov due to chazakah of creditor's possession
Distinction between issur and mamona regarding application of rov principle
Explanation of Shmuel's view using creditor's presumption and burden of proof
Tosafot's difficulty: how can rov be sufficient for issur but not for mamona if life is more precious

macro_segments.title: Case of widow/divorcee claiming ketubah status and role of rov with witnesses and kol

micro_segments.title: Case: woman divorced or widowed without ketubah seeks to prove status for payment amount
Proof by witnesses to hinuma, uncovered hair, and wedding accoutrements indicating betula status
Gemara's question: why not rely on rov that most women marry as betulot
Ravina's answer: rov applies only when there is kol but absence of kol undermines rov presumption
Gemara's clarification that kol and edim are distinct: absence of kol still requires edim
Gemara's resolution: not all betulot have kol but rov do, so absence of kol undermines rov weight
